seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Secret Harbour and Serpentine. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Secret Harbour vs Serpentine: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Secret Harbour and Serpentine.
heading: Secret Harbour vs Serpentine — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Secret Harbour, Western Australia and Serpentine, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Serpentine has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Secret Harbour (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Secret Harbour or Serpentine safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Secret Harbour has a flood risk score of 29/100, while Serpentine scores 49/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Secret Harbour scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Serpentine scores 26/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Secret Harbour or Serpentine?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Serpentine has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
